metadata.title: The Plan-Review Checklist Before You Click Approve
metadata.description_blurb: Clicking approve because a plan sounds reasonable is not supervision — it is abdication. The checklist is what turns a glance into a gate.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

beats.narration_text: Kenji approved the plan in twenty seconds. Six steps, clean prose, read well at a glance. Step four deleted the originals. There was no backup step before step four. Twelve files gone. Not because Cowork was wrong to execute the plan — because Kenji approved a plan he did not read carefully enough to catch step four. The checklist exists for that twenty seconds. This is Liam, in for Bear.
beats.new_visual_element: ClaudeComposerAsk — cold open, plan review for grant folder organization

props.command: Review this Cowork plan for organizing a grant files folder

beats.narration_text: Check one: does any step modify, rename, or delete originals? If yes, is there a backup step before it? This is the most common missing element in Cowork plans. 'Delete duplicates' is a write operation. 'Move to archive' is a write operation. 'Rename for consistency' is a write operation. Every write needs a recovery path before it executes.
beats.new_visual_element: Risky step check — modify/rename/delete requires backup step before

beats.narration_text: Check two: is there a step where a human sees the output before it becomes final? Not a review after everything is done — a gate in the plan, mid-execution, where the plan pauses and you see what was produced. If every step runs to completion before you see anything, verification is not in the plan.
beats.new_visual_element: Missing verification gate — mid-plan pause vs end-of-plan review
beats.spark_line: Gate in the plan, not at the end.

beats.narration_text: If the plan has a problem, do not approve and hope. Redirect. The redirect form: 'Do not act yet. Add a backup step after step three that copies originals to a dated folder before any deletion. Then resubmit the plan.' Redirect is not rejection — it is a specification of what the plan needs to become before you will approve it.
beats.new_visual_element: Redirect form — do not act yet / specific addition required

beats.narration_text: Five points. Risky step check, missing verification gate, access scope check, stop condition check, and the question: could you explain every step of this plan to someone who needs to undo it? Approval without the checklist is a guess. Approval with the checklist is supervision.
Run the checklist on your next Cowork plan before you approve. Paste this.

props.command: Review this Cowork plan against five checks: (1) any step that modifies, renames, or deletes originals — is there a backup step before it? (2) is there a human verification gate mid-plan, not just at the end? (3) does any step access data outside what the brief authorized? (4) are stop conditions named for unexpected states? (5) could you explain every step to someone who needs to undo it? Flag any failures before I approve. [paste plan here]

beats.narration_text: If this saves twelve files, subscribe. Liam, in for Bear.
